content.content:
content: '# Web Document


  ## Metadata


  - title: Booking Accessible Tickets | The O2

  - url: https://www.theo2.co.uk/accessibility/booking

  - source: The O2

  - domain: theo2.co.uk


  ## Content


  # Booking Accessible Tickets


  ## Access Card


  The O2 works with Nimbus Disability and uses the Access Card to communicate access
  requirements for events at The O2 arena. Guests booking accessible tickets are directed
  to the venue''s Access Card information for details about the registration system.


  ## Personal Assistants


  A Personal Assistant (PA) ticket has a specific eligibility condition. To book a
  PA ticket, the guest must have a valid Nimbus Disability Access Card with the `+1`
  symbol. The PA ticket must be chosen at the same time as the guest''s own ticket.


  Guests who require more than one Personal Assistant and have the relevant symbol
  on their Nimbus Disability Access Card are asked to contact the dedicated access
  line on 020 8463 3359.


  ## Accessible and General Tickets


  When a booking needs both accessible tickets and general tickets, the two ticket
  types must be booked separately. Guests should follow the purchase flow and select
  the relevant ticket or seat type for each booking.


  The page also links to a virtual map showing the location of wheelchair accessible
  platforms.


  ## BSL Interpreting Service


  The O2 provides information about its SignVideo British Sign Language interpreting
  service. SignVideo is available from 10am to 2pm, Monday to Friday. The page also
  lists the Typetext number 18001 020 8463 3359.

  '
metadata: {}
title: Booking Accessible Tickets | The O2
url: https://www.theo2.co.uk/accessibility/booking

content: "# Web Document\n\n## Metadata\n\n- title: Nimbus Access Card at The O2 |\
  \ Accessibility Support\n- url: https://www.theo2.co.uk/accessibility/nimbus-access-card/\n\
  - source: The O2\n- domain: theo2.co.uk\n\n## Content\n\n# Nimbus Access Card at\
  \ The O2\n\nThe O2 explains that its official ticketing partner, AXS, works with\
  \ Nimbus Disability and the Access Card system to communicate access requirements\
  \ for disabled guests.\n\nOnce an Access Card registration is active, guests can\
  \ browse events at The O2 arena and book accessible tickets through the AXS Accessible\
  \ Tickets site. The venue says the online system is intended to provide clear ticket\
  \ options and real-time availability without requiring guests to contact the team\
  \ separately for every accessible booking.\n\nThe system is also designed to reduce\
  \ repeated disclosure. Guests supply their access information through the registration\
  \ process rather than carrying supporting documents or repeatedly explaining their\
  \ circumstances to venue staff.\n\n## Registration Options\n\nThe page describes\
  \ two registration routes.\n\nA free Access Registration allows an account to be\
  \ recognised on AXS.com for shows at The O2 arena. A guest who already has an Access\
  \ Card can enter their details when prompted during the ticket purchase flow.\n\n\
  A full Access Card costs \xA315 for three years. It includes the same access to\
  \ The O2 booking system as the free registration, together with wider benefits such\
  \ as recognition at participating venues, a photo ID card with accessibility symbols,\
  \ an online access directory, and other membership features. The page describes\
  \ possible companion-ticket benefits as one of the wider benefits that may be available\
  \ at participating venues.\n\n## What the Access Card Does\n\nThe Access Card translates\
  \ disability or impairment-related barriers into symbols that communicate the reasonable\
  \ adjustments a person may need. The aim is to let providers understand relevant\
  \ support needs without requiring the guest to disclose more personal detail each\
  \ time.\n\nNimbus Disability manages the registration scheme. After registration,\
  \ Nimbus provides a unique registration number that can be used to link access needs\
  \ into The O2 and AXS ticketing system.\n\nGuests who cannot use online forms or\
  \ online sales because of the nature of their impairment still need to register.\
  \ The page says Nimbus can assist with telephone registration on 0330 808 5108.\n\
  \n## Booking Accessible Tickets Online\n\nAccessible tickets for events at The O2\
  \ arena can be bought online. Because event on-sales may attract significant demand,\
  \ the venue advises guests to use online accessible booking rather than relying\
  \ on the phone line.\n\nGuests with access requirements must register those requirements\
  \ through the Access Card system before booking through AXS. Once linked, the registration\
  \ allows the ticketing system to present ticket options connected to the recorded\
  \ access requirements, subject to ticket availability.\n\n## Booking by Phone\n\n\
  A Nimbus Access Card is also required for accessible ticket bookings made through\
  \ the dedicated phone line. Guests who need more than two tickets, including a Personal\
  \ Assistant ticket, are asked to call the accessible bookings team on 020 8463 3359.\
  \ The team is available 9am to 5pm, Monday to Friday, and the line is for accessible\
  \ ticket bookings only.\n\nThe page also states that PA-ticket refunds are not available\
  \ after an event has taken place or for tickets bought outside the accessible ticket\
  \ portal.\n"
metadata: {}
title: Nimbus Access Card at The O2 | Accessibility Support
url: https://www.theo2.co.uk/accessibility/nimbus-access-card/

Your current The O2 accessibility registration is active and recognised for **Wheelchair Access** and **Level Access**. You should therefore be offered **wheelchair-accessible/level-access seating** through the AXS accessible-ticketing process, subject to availability.

For the person accompanying you:

- The O2’s **Personal Assistant (PA) ticket** is available only where your Nimbus Access Card has the **“+1” symbol**.
- Your current registration does **not** show a **+1** symbol, so the day-to-day helper would currently need to purchase a **standard accompanying ticket**.
- If you believe you should have PA recognition, contact Nimbus Disability to update or review your registration before booking.
- If you need more than two tickets, including a PA ticket, The O2’s accessible bookings line is **020 8463 3359**.

Book both tickets together through The O2/AXS accessible-ticketing route where possible:  
[Booking Accessible Tickets – The O2](https://www.theo2.co.uk/accessibility/booking)

final_answer:
Your current The O2 accessibility registration is active and recognised for **Wheelchair Access** and **Level Access**. You should therefore be offered **wheelchair-accessible/level-access seating** through the AXS accessible-ticketing process, subject to availability.

For the person accompanying you:

- The O2’s **Personal Assistant (PA) ticket** is available only where your Nimbus Access Card has the **“+1” symbol**.
- Your current registration does **not** show a **+1** symbol, so the day-to-day helper would currently need to purchase a **standard accompanying ticket**.
- If you believe you should have PA recognition, contact Nimbus Disability to update or review your registration before booking.
- If you need more than two tickets, including a PA ticket, The O2’s accessible bookings line is **020 8463 3359**.

Book both tickets together through The O2/AXS accessible-ticketing route where possible:  
[Booking Accessible Tickets – The O2](https://www.theo2.co.uk/accessibility/booking)

notes: utility is tool-subsequence or required-tools based when configured
tool results/errors are message-aware when messages are supplied
security and help checks are risk-spec based
parameter-level forbidden calls support args/results and count limits
document access is counted only for read/open tools

(?=

)
(?=
